name: Patout's Boiled Crawfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the crawfish thoroughly and remove any fish bones or debris.
2. Fill a large stockpot about one-quarter full with water.
3. Add salt and peppers to the water and bring it to a boil.
4. Add whole onions, corn, and potatoes (if using a cloth sack, they will be easy to remove later).
5. Return to a boil, then lower heat to medium and simmer for 8 minutes.
6. Add crawfish, cover, and raise heat to high.
7. Cook for an additional 7 minutes until steam escapes from under the lid.
8. Remove from heat and let sit for 4 minutes before opening the lid.
9. Transfer onions, corn, potatoes, and cooked crawfish to a bowl; drain the crawfish.
10. In a separate container, mix sprinkled seasonings (salt, white pepper, red pepper, black pepper) with the crawfish until well coated.
11. Cover and let sit for an additional 7 minutes.
12. Serve immediately with the vegetables on a large table covered with paper towels. When everyone is finished, they "peel for the house."
13. The peeled tails can be used in cold crawfish cocktail or salad or for Fried Crawfish the next day. Serves 8-10.
